title: Nebulon Ranger

{{Quote|<nowiki />''Nebulon Ranger'', you are flying in the company of our mortal enemies. Heave to, or we'll blast you from the sky!|[[Novar (Human)|Novar]], before the Battle of Iziz|<ref name="audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
The '''''Nebulon Ranger''''' was a modified [[Coruscant-class Heavy Courier|''Coruscant''-class Heavy Courier]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that belonged to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Cay Qel-Droma]]. Originally an ordinary [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]], the ''Ranger'' was modified extensively by Qel-Droma, who transformed it into what he considered a very trustworthy vessel. In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], Qel-Droma, his brother [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]] were sent to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] to bring peace to the long-lasting [[Beast Wars]] planetary conflict. After traveling across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] world, the three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were enlisted to retrieve Onderonian [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]]'s [[Raid on the Iziz Royal Palace|kidnapped]] [[Galia|daughter]]. In the search for the missing [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] over the planet's [[Tree/Legends|forests]], the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was shot down by a [[seeker-torpedo]] fired by the [[Beast Riders]], a group of [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] dissidents fighting [[Iziz ruling family|Onderon's monarchy]]. The damage was repairable and after discovering that Princess Galia had staged the kidnapping with the Beast Riders to escape the monarchy's use of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], the Jedi joined the Beast Riders' side. Once fully repaired, the ''Ranger'' headed for the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] with the intention of negotiating for peace. Although Amanoa rejected the occupant's overtures, she and her [[Royal Armed Forces|army]] were ultimately [[Battle of Iziz|defeated]] in the final battle of the Beast Wars, during which the ''Nebulon Ranger'' saw minimal action.

The ''Nebulon Ranger'' experienced continued service over the next several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], including an errand to bring [[Vultan]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Oss Wilum]] to Onderon in [[3998 BBY]]. [[3997 BBY|The following year]], the ''Ranger'' was involved in a [[Mission to Empress Teta|mission]] to the planet [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]]. Ulic Qel-Droma had gone [[Spy/Legends|undercover]] in the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], a [[Sith cult/Legends|dark-side sect]] that had [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|taken control]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. However, after worries that Qel-Droma was in too deep, the ''Ranger'' transported Cay Qel-Droma, Tott Doneeta, and fellow Jedi [[Nomi Sunrider]] to Empress Teta, where Sunrider descended in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], hoping that she could find a way to contact Ulic on the planet below. However, when she was forced to escape from a Krath [[Prison/Legends|prison]], Cay and Doneeta swooped in on the ''Ranger'', blasting attacking Krath [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and rescuing Sunrider. Despite their best efforts, Ulic ultimately refused to join them, and by [[3996 BBY]], had become a full-fledged [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] under the [[Sith training|tutelage]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]. Together they [[Great Sith War/Legends|waged war]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and after the [[Battle of Kemplex Nine]] caused a [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]]; the shock wave from the nova headed directly toward the [[Jedi academy]] world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. During the scramble to salvage as much [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi knowledge]] as possible before the shock wave hit, Ulic Qel-Droma and Exar Kun [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|descended upon Ossus]] in order to steal [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi artifacts]] for their own purposes. Their arrival was noticed by Cay, who went up in the ''Nebulon Ranger'' in order to meet his brother. However, things escalated, and the ''Nebulon Ranger'' ended up getting shot down after a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]] between the two brothers. Several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, the shock wave destroyed the remains of the ''Ranger''.

==Description==
{{Quote|Hit the shields, Tott!|Cay Qel-Droma to Tott Doneeta, while a seeker-torpedo streaked toward the ''Nebulon Ranger''; as the ship's shield generators were a major power drain, the deflector shields could not be left on all the time|<ref name="beastwars1">[[Tales of the Jedi 1|''Tales of the Jedi'' 1]]</ref>}}
[[File:NebulonRanger_schem.jpg|thumb|left|180px|''Nebulon Ranger'' schematics]]
A [[Coruscant-class Heavy Courier|''Coruscant''-class Heavy Courier]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] manufactured by [[Krasten Shipyards]], the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was relatively small and had a length of just twenty-five [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. It was well-armed, however, boasting four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], two [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|launchers]],<ref name="potj" /> two [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launchers and a [[pulse-wave cannon]], all of which were controlled from a [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] station in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]].<ref name="egvv" /> Each concussion missile launcher could hold up to eight missiles each,<ref name="potj" /> while the proton torpedo launchers could hold up to six. The ''Nebulon Ranger'' featured eight [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generators]] that provided full [[Hull/Legends|hull]] protection. However, they were a great power drain, and therefore the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] were raised only in combat. Thus, the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was highly vulnerable to a surprise attack.<ref name="egvv" /> The ''Nebulon Ranger'' could hold up to twenty [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo, and in addition could hold enough consumables to last the crew two [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] required two crewmembers and could accommodate four passengers.<ref name="potj" /> The ''Ranger'' was a multi-sectioned ship, with the two main series of [[Engine/Legends|engine]] arrays located on a right-angle frame, allowing the ship an easy way to separate the engines and granting better maneuverability without the need for [[Maneuvering jet/Legends|maneuvering jets]].<ref name="egvv" />

The ship featured retractable wings that were typically extended during subspace flight but retracted during landing. The heavily [[Armor/Legends|armored]] main wing contained the fuel tanks, which fed into the ship's six [[AlderaanMotors/Legends|AlderaanMotors]] [[Power generator/Legends|power generators]] and engines. It also held the ''Ranger''{{'s}} [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] and a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] pylon. Each wingtip featured a retractable, rotating [[Laser/Legends|laser]] cannon, and a more powerful cannon was located at the base of the second engine array. The cockpit was in between the ship's forward mandibles, and opened into the living and cargo sections in the center. The main [[Entry ramp/Legends|boarding ramp]] and emergency escape hatch were located behind the cockpit, and three [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] were mounted below the pulse cannon. There was also an escape hatch located on the top of the ship directly behind the main canopy. The ''Ranger'' had a cargo bay that was sectioned into several compartments, and contained [[Life support/Legends|life support]], [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], and [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] controls. Smaller vehicles could be kept in the bay, and the ship featured a drop bay between the engines to deploy them as quickly as possible. To this end, short-range [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] were often kept in the cargo bay.<ref name="egvv" /> The ''Nebulon Ranger'' was modified extensively by its owner, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Cay Qel-Droma]]. Qel-Droma fitted the ''Ranger'' with a better [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and upgraded the [[Power coupling/Legends|power couplings]] for faster [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speed.<ref name="potj" />

==History==
===The Beast Wars of Onderon===
{{Quote|I'm ''not'' taking you with me Ulic… because I'm staying here. ''You'' are going to Onderon!|Arca Jeth|<ref name="beastwars1" />}}
Originally an ordinary [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]], the ''Nebulon Ranger'' eventually fell into the hands of Jedi Knight Cay Qel-Droma. Qel-Droma spent a great deal of his spare [[Time/Legends|time]] tinkering with the ship and making many modifications, including the addition of a new hyperdrive and upgrades to the power couplings for better sublight speed.<ref name="potj" /> At the time, Qel-Droma, along with his brother [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and their [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] friend [[Tott Doneeta]], was an [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]]. In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], the three were [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] when Jeth gave them a mission: to travel to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], a war-torn world that was embroiled in a long-running [[Beast Wars|conflict]] between the aristocracy of the walled [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]] and [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] criminals known as the [[Beast Riders]], and bring peace. The group departed at once in the ''Nebulon Ranger'', traveling across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to Onderon. The ''Ranger'' crossed the [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]] and circumnavigated the planet, finally coming to within viewing distance of Iziz. However, on the final approach to Iziz the ''Ranger'' was attacked by a group of Beast Riders on their flying [[drexl]] mounts, and although Doneeta prepared to fire on the exiles, Ulic Qel-Droma refused to allow him to retaliate. Instead, they flew the ship right through the Beast Rider swarm toward Iziz without firing a shot, and finally landed at the [[Iziz Royal Palace]] after receiving covering fire from several [[Iziz defense tower]]s on the city walls.<ref name="beastwars1" />

[[File:NebulonRangerExplosion.jpg|thumb|left|225px|The ''Nebulon Ranger'' is hit by a Beast Rider seeker-torpedo above Onderon.]]
The three Jedi went to meet with [[Amanoa]], [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of Onderon, and her daughter [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]], but during their meeting the same group of Beast Riders that attacked the ''Nebulon Ranger'' on its approach to Iziz [[Raid on the Iziz Royal Palace|broke into]] the palace and kidnapped the Princess. Vowing to retrieve her, the Jedi followed the fleeing Beast Riders in the ''Nebulon Ranger''. However, the Beast Riders fired a [[seeker-torpedo]] at the ship, which hit the ''Ranger'' before Tott Doneeta could activate the deflector shields.<ref name="beastwars1" /> The torpedo caused major damage to the ''Ranger'', which went into a downward spiral, and with the lateral fins not responding and the systems [[Computer/Legends|computer]] down, the ship crash-landed in Onderon's [[Tree/Legends|forests]]. However, the Jedi used the deflector shields to cushion the impact with the planet's surface, and although the lateral fins were damaged and the sublight [[transponder]] was gone, the damage was reparable. Going in search of the Beast Riders, they ended up discovering that Queen Amanoa and [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] of Onderon were [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cultists]] that [[Naddist|worshiped]] [[Death/Legends|dead]] former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]]. The Jedi worked alongside the Beast Riders to formulate a plan to end the Beast Wars—Galia and [[Beast-Lord]] [[Oron Kira]], whom she had run away to [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia|marry]], would join the Jedi on the ''Nebulon Ranger'' and fly to Iziz to try to convince Queen Amanoa to peacefully end the war. If that failed, Oron Kira's father [[Modon Kira|Modon]] would lead a great [[Beast Rider army]] against Iziz.<ref name="beastwars2" />

Cay Qel-Droma was able to repair the ''Nebulon Ranger'' in less than a day, and the ''Ranger'' soon took flight for Iziz, with Galia and Oron Kira on board and followed by a Beast Rider escort. However, the Beast Riders piqued the attention of the Iziz defense guns, which immediately fired upon the ship and its escort. Cay Qel-Droma frantically contacted the Iziz Royal Palace, telling them that they had Princess Galia in tow, which prompted the guns to hold their fire and allow the ''Nebulon Ranger'' to land. Queen Amanoa rejected their peaceful overtures, and the Beast Riders subsequently attacked Iziz. The exile [[Army/Legends|army]] won the battle, assisted by the sudden arrival of a [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]–wielding Arca Jeth. Queen Amanoa died at the conclusion of the battle, her dark-side power having fled her; Oron Kira and Galia soon consecrated their [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] under the laws of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], ascending to the positions of [[King/Legends|King]] and Queen of Onderon respectively.<ref name="beastwars2" />

===Encounter with the Krath===
{{Quote|Good luck Nomi. Give us a ''signal'' when you're ready to be picked up… or if you need some ''firepower''. We'll hide the ''Nebulon Ranger'' in the shadow of the Tetan Moon.|Cay Qel-Droma, after launching Nomi Sunrider onto Empress Teta in an escape pod|<ref name="darklords5">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]]</ref>}}
[[File:RangerTeta.jpg|thumb|right|325px|The ''Nebulon Ranger'' battles Krath soldiers on Empress Teta.]]
In [[3998 BBY]], the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was involved in another conflict. The [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]], where Naddist cult members went into open rebellion, threw Iziz into turmoil. Hoping to reduce the dark-side presence still in the city, King Oron Kira and Queen Galia led a grand [[Funeral of Amanoa|funeral procession]] through Iziz's streets, after which the sarcophagi of both [[Freedon Nadd's sarcophagus|Freedon Nadd]] and Amanoa would be moved to a [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|new tomb]] on the Dxun moon. The ''Nebulon Ranger'' was used to bring in other Jedi to quell the Naddist uprising, specifically the [[Vultan]] [[Oss Wilum]], who was flown in from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] by Tott Doneeta. The ''Ranger'' landed during the funeral procession, and was left empty when Doneeta and Wilum left to meet the other Jedi present, including Master Jeth and the Qel-Droma brothers.<ref name="nadd1">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 1]]</ref>

Although the Freedon Nadd Uprising was soon put down,<ref name="nadd2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]]</ref> by [[3997 BBY|the next year]] the Jedi faced a new threat, and the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was put into action yet again. A Sith cult known as the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] rose to power in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]], and after an [[Battle of Deneba|attack]] on a Jedi [[Conclave at Deneba|conclave]] which left Master Arca Jeth dead, Ulic Qel-Droma volunteered to go to [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] and infiltrate the Krath, hoping to destroy it from inside. However, Qel-Droma's friends and fellow Jedi, still in possession of the ''Nebulon Ranger'', began to grow worried about his well-being, and decided to rescue him. Cay Qel-Droma, Tott Doneeta, and fellow Jedi [[Nomi Sunrider]], who was romantically involved with Ulic, subsequently [[Mission to Empress Teta|journeyed]] to Empress Teta in the ''Nebulon Ranger''. They launched Sunrider onto the planet below in an escape pod, hoping that she could find a way to contact the wayward Qel-Droma. After the ejection of the escape pod, Qel-Droma and Doneeta hid the ''Ranger'' in the shadow of the [[Atale/Legends|Tetan Moon]], waiting for a signal from Sunrider. Sunrider was soon captured by the Krath, but escaped; grabbing a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] from a fallen guard, she told Doneeta and Qel-Droma to come and rescue Ulic. The ''Nebulon Ranger'' came in from the Tetan Moon at top speed, killing several Krath [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with its laser cannons and finally landing in the streets of the Tetan capital city, [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]], near Sunrider's location. Although they were able to come in contact with Ulic, he refused to join them, and even attacked them with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]; pursued by dozens of Krath soldiers and ships, they were forced to abandon Ulic and flee to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the ''Nebulon Ranger''. They subsequently returned to the Jedi world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] in the ship to determine their next step.<ref name="darklords5" />

===Destruction===
{{Quote|Ulic! Stop! I'm going down!|Cay Qel-Droma, after the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was damaged in a dogfight with Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="sithwar5" />}}
[[File:RangerCrashes.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The ''Nebulon Ranger'' crashes on Ossus.]]
The ''Nebulon Ranger'' was called into action again in [[3996 BBY]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], where Ulic Qel-Droma, now a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], and his [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] led an army against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. That year, a [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]] caused by the destruction of the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] of [[Star/Legends|stars]] during the [[Battle of Kemplex Nine]] created a shock wave that headed directly for Ossus. Knowing that the shock wave would kill all life on the planet and destroy Ossus' famed [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]], the Jedi present, including Cay Qel-Droma, Tott Doneeta, and Nomi Sunrider, tried to save as much Jedi knowledge and [[Jedi artifact/Legends|artifacts]] as they could in what little time they had. The ''Nebulon Ranger'' was one of many ships participating in this evacuation. However, Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma descended on Ossus soon after, with the intent of stealing artifacts for themselves. Their arrival was witnessed by Ulic's brother Cay, who could [[Force sense/Legends|sense]] Ulic's presence in [[Cay's Dream|his ship]]. Cay sprinted for the ''Nebulon Ranger'' and took to the skies, but Ulic was not receptive to his brother's greetings and tried to evade Cay's pursuit. However, Cay followed, targeting laser strikes against his brother's engines. Ulic proved the better [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] on that day, however, slipping behind the ''Ranger'' and hitting it with several precise blasts. Severely damaged and at risk for explosion, the ''Nebulon Ranger'' crashed onto the planet's surface, but Cay was able to get out alive.<ref name="sithwar5" />

Although Cay Qel-Droma's deft piloting preserved his life, the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was almost totally destroyed. His brother Ulic soon came upon the scene, and the two brothers ended up [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueling]] with their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. However, Ulic proved too much for Cay, and Cay was slain near the remains of the ''Ranger''. Several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, the shock wave from the Cron Supernova reached Ossus, consigning the wreckage of the ''Nebulon Ranger'' to the same fate as everything else on Ossus.<ref name="sithwar5" /> The wreckage was not completely destroyed however. The crumbling remains were still visible outside the Jedi Temple on Ossus during Luke Skywalker's exploration of the ruins during the events of the Dark Empire trilogy<ref name="Empire's End 1">[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]</ref>. A lasting monument to the tragedy of the Qel Droma brothers. 

==Commanders and crew==
{{Quote|But ''I'' can fix it.|Cay Qel-Droma|<ref name="beastwars2" />}}
The ''Nebulon Ranger'' belonged to Cay Qel-Droma, who heavily modified the ship, transforming it from an ordinary space transport to a trusty vessel fit for a Jedi. Qel-Droma treated the ship as his most prized possession, spending much of his free time modifying it.<ref name="potj" /> As such, he held an intimate knowledge of the ship's inner workings and could make major repairs in very little time, as demonstrated after the ''Ranger'' was hit by a seeker-torpedo on Onderon. His brother Ulic flew the ship often as well, including during their mission to Onderon.<ref name="beastwars1" /> The Qel-Droma brothers were not the only ones to utilize the ''Nebulon Ranger'', however, as Tott Doneeta flew the ship by himself at least once.<ref name="nadd1" /> Cay Qel-Droma was the last to ever operate the ''Nebulon Ranger'' above Ossus in 3996 BBY,<ref name="sithwar5" /> shortly before its destruction by the Cron Supernova's shock wave.<ref name="potj" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Nebulon Ranger'' first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon|''Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon'']], the first story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released in [[1993]].<ref name="beastwars1" /><ref name="beastwars2" /> The ''Ranger'' appeared in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising|''Freedon Nadd Uprising'']],<ref name="nadd1" /> [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']],<ref name="darklords5" /> and [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War|''The Sith War'']] story arcs.<ref name="sithwar5" /> The ship was also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio adaptation]]<ref name="audio" /> and the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]].<ref name="darklordsaudio">[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]</ref> The ''Ranger'' was drawn by three separate artists: [[Chris Gossett]]<ref name="beastwars1" /><ref name="beastwars2" /><ref name="darklords5" />—who originally designed the ship<ref name="interview">[[Wookieepedia:Interview/Christian Gossett|Interview with Christian Gossett]]</ref>—[[Tony Akins]],<ref name="nadd1" /> and [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="sithwar5" /> Further information on the ''Nebulon Ranger''{{'s}} history and technical layout was given in ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'',<ref name="egvv" /> the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'',<ref name="companion">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> and the ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''.<ref name="potj" /> In [[2008]], the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|II|365-366|Nebulon Ranger}}</ref>

*[[Tales of the Jedi 2|''Tales of the Jedi'' 2]]
